source_path: ferre-rag-model/Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1987 SS/Womenswear/Fashion show drawings/41460.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
description: Long skirt in white cotton piqué with dark blue 4 cm wide stripes (20 cm spacing). In reality, forming this skirt are two fully overlapping pieces of cloth – one the piqué, the other a plain silk organza – softly gathered at the waist. Open at mid front, all the way down to hem. Short skirt in dark blue wool/silk. Slim line, knee length. Top in dark blue wool/silk with black glass tube beading. Slim fit, round neck. Totally open back with crisscrossing ribbon straps. Belt in white piqué with rectangular chrome buckle.
materials: Cotton piqué in horizontal stripe print (Sisan, Milano; art. 803, des. 1055), wool/silk satin (Taroni, Como; art. 17055, col. blue), silk organza, glass tube beads
present_location: FGF space c/o Open Care - Milan
remark: The volume of the long skirt captures both a once-upon-a-time majestic feel and an unknown airy lightness. Here too it’s all about the artistry of the construction process, in memory of Marcel Boucher and his enchanting creations.
working_process: All over glass tube beading
llm_description: Illustration of a three-piece evening dress featuring a black top, a short blue skirt, and a long white skirt with blue horizontal stripes, accented by a white belt.

source_path: ferre-rag-model/Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1987 SS/Womenswear/Fashion show photos/19435.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
description: Long skirt in white cotton piqué with dark blue 4 cm wide stripes (20 cm spacing). In reality, forming this skirt are two fully overlapping pieces of cloth – one the piqué, the other a plain silk organza – softly gathered at the waist. Open at mid front, all the way down to hem. Short skirt in dark blue wool/silk. Slim line, knee length. Top in dark blue wool/silk with black glass tube beading. Slim fit, round neck. Totally open back with crisscrossing ribbon straps. Belt in white piqué with rectangular chrome buckle.
materials: Cotton piqué in horizontal stripe print (Sisan, Milano; art. 803, des. 1055), wool/silk satin (Taroni, Como; art. 17055, col. blue), silk organza, glass tube beads
present_location: FGF space c/o Open Care - Milan
remark: The volume of the long skirt captures both a once-upon-a-time majestic feel and an unknown airy lightness. Here too it’s all about the artistry of the construction process, in memory of Marcel Boucher and his enchanting creations.
working_process: All over glass tube beading
llm_description: A three-piece evening dress featuring a sequined top, a short dark blue skirt, and a long white skirt with blue horizontal stripes, cinched with a wide white belt and a blue bow detail.

source_path: ferre-rag-model/Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1988 SS/Womenswear/Fashion show drawings/34351.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
description: Jacket in red wool satin. Singlebreasted, hip skimming. Bell shape in front, trumpet cut in back. High collar with rim in black wool satin. Boxy shoulders, radial arm construction. Long sleeves with black wool satin rim. Skirt in white wool satin with vertical stripes in black velvet ribbon. Eight-part construction. Slim line, high waist, knee length. Shirt in white silk gazar. Crossover style with three-button side closure. Mannish collar, no sleeves, (upper) hip-skimming length. Double scarf in scotch plaid silk taffeta (black, blue, red, green, yellow).
materials: Wool satin, silk gazar, cotton velvet ribbon, silk taffeta
remark: A daytime jacket vaguely reminiscent of a tailcoat. The dandyish hint of eccentricity comes through mostly in the stripe & plaid mix.
working_process: Application in parallel stripes (with 1.5 cm spacing) of silk velvet ribbon on wool satin fabric
llm_description: Fashion illustration of a three-piece outfit including a red jacket with black trim, a striped skirt, and a scarf.

source_path: ferre-rag-model/Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1988 SS/Womenswear/Fashion show drawings/34362.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
description: Shirt in scotch plaid silk taffeta (black, red, avian blue, green, yellow). Crossover style, no fastening. Mannish collar. Long wide sleeves with bias-cut corolla cuffs. Big open pleat from neck to mid back. Skirt in scotch plaid silk taffeta (black, red, avian blue, green, yellow). Plaid cotton doubling (red, green, yellow, black). Circular cut, knee length. Hand topstitching. Ascot in white silk gazar.
materials: Silk taffeta, cotton cloth, silk gazar
remark: Here attention goes primarily to the robust fabric (thanks to both doubling and topstitching) and to the natural sense of shape and volume, very much a matter of cut.
working_process: Extensive 1 cm parallel topstitching in sync with the skirt’s circular cut
llm_description: Fashion illustration of a two-piece outfit featuring a plaid top and skirt, rendered with bold strokes and color accents.

label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
description: Shirt in scotch plaid silk taffeta (black, red, avian blue, green, yellow). Crossover style, no fastening. Mannish collar. Long wide sleeves with bias-cut corolla cuffs. Big open pleat from neck to mid back. Skirt in scotch plaid silk taffeta (black, red, avian blue, green, yellow). Plaid cotton doubling (red, green, yellow, black). Circular cut, knee length. Hand topstitching. Ascot in white silk gazar.
materials: Silk taffeta, cotton cloth, silk gazar
remark: Here attention goes primarily to the robust fabric (thanks to both doubling and topstitching) and to the natural sense of shape and volume, very much a matter of cut.
working_process: Extensive 1 cm parallel topstitching in sync with the skirt’s circular cut
llm_description: A two-piece outfit featuring a plaid top and circular skirt, accessorized with a white ascot, red gloves, and a decorative bow at the waist.

source_path: ferre-rag-model/Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1988 SS/Womenswear/Fashion show drawings/34368.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
description: Jacket in striking white&black stripe tubular wool. Slim mid thigh-skimming line with simple one-button closure opening toward bottom. Long sleeves with radial arm construction. Straight slit neckline slanting upwards. Skirt in red tubular wool. Six-part construction. Slim line, knee length.
exhibitions: “Ferré. Abito come accento che esce dal corpo”, area Bernardelli, Mantova, 2015
materials: Tubular wool
present_location: FGF space c/o Open Care - Milan
remark: The mannish inspiration behind this daytime jacket is offset by some distinct sartorial maneuvers: namely, the elimination of collar & lapels and the creation of a flare effect in front. The size and positioning of the stripes are clearly designed to make a style statement.
llm_description: Fashion illustration of a two-piece outfit featuring a striped jacket and a red skirt.

source_path: ferre-rag-model/Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1988 SS/Womenswear/Fashion show drawings/34396.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
description: Dress all in silk georgette with zigzagging silk tape overprinted in a red/yellow/green/sky blue/lilac/black floral motif. Form- fitting two-piece construction: strapless top (diff. drawing); knee-length skirt. Red suede belt with rectangular buckle to match.
materials: Silk tape, silk georgette, cotton piqué
present_location: FGF space c/o Open Care - Milan
remark: True to Ferré tradition, here the simplicity is only apparent. What seems to be a plisse print fabric is actually the result of a lengthy process where silk tape is first sewn on by hand or machine and then lavishly overprinted. What seems to be no more than a skinny bustier-dress captures in back a flowers-fluttering-in-the-wind allure thanks to the rich decorative elements.
working_process: Silk tape sewn onto silk georgette and then overprinted
llm_description: Fashion illustration of a two-piece dress with a bustier top and knee-length skirt, featuring a floral motif and zigzagging silk tape.

source_path: ferre-rag-model/Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1988 SS/Womenswear/Fashion show drawings/72978.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
description: Knee-length dress with inner structure consisting of a strapless eight-segment seven-whalebone bustier. Horizontal topstitching with 1 cm spacing, zip closure at mid back. Sewn onto the bustier is a red/yellow/green/blue/lilac/black floral print gazar minidress with silk jersey lining. The outer part consists of five circular pieces of fabric – three all in gazar, two offset by crinoline – forming a rich swirl effect in front thanks to the plissé motif. 10 cm gazar hem. Small snap-buttons for the closure in back.
materials: Silk gazar: nylon crinoline; silk jersey; cotton cloth
present_location: FGF space c/o Open Care - Milan
remark: Sparking sensations of levity and motion, this dress has a sculptural feel with all the fullness in front. The gazar's firmness contributes to ensuring the plasticity.
working_process: Parallel topstitching with 1 cm spacing
llm_description: Fashion illustration of a knee-length dress with a strapless bustier and a flared skirt, featuring a floral print and a swirl effect in the front.

The outer part consists of five circular pieces of fabric – three all in gazar, two offset by crinoline – forming a rich swirl effect in front thanks to the plissé motif. 10 cm gazar hem. Small snap-buttons for the closure in back.
materials: Silk gazar: nylon crinoline; silk jersey; cotton cloth
present_location: FGF space c/o Open Care - Milan
remark: Sparking sensations of levity and motion, this dress has a sculptural feel with all the fullness in front. The gazar's firmness contributes to ensuring the plasticity.
working_process: Parallel topstitching with 1 cm spacing
llm_description: A strapless, knee-length dress with a floral print, featuring a bustier and a flared skirt with a swirl effect.

source_path: ferre-rag-model/Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1988 SS/Womenswear/Fashion show drawings/34399.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (scritta oro)
object: Completo composto da camicia e gonna
description: Camicia di natté di seta nero, di linea diritta che si chiude a sormonto, con collo montante. Al centro schiena un cannone di cm. 6. Manica ampia a kimono, lunghezza al gomito, trattenuta con quattro pieghe da un polsino sbieco chiuso con un bottone ricoperto. Lunghezza primo fianco. Completamente doppiata con lo stesso tessuto. Gonna di piquet di cotone a righe bianco-rosso, composta da due pannelli uniti a formare una ruota intera la cui ampiezza è completamente riportata sul davanti con motivo a pieghe, con ampie volute. Il volume della gonna è sorretto da una crinolina con orlo di cm. 28. Piccoli bottoni e gancio in metallo chiudono la gonna sul dietro. Lunghezza alla caviglia con orlo asimmetrico,più lungo sul dietro Cintura di colore bianco e nero in canneté di seta con fibbia ricoperta.
exhibitions: “Ferré. Abito come accento che esce dal corpo”, area Bernardelli, Mantova, 2015; Storie di Moda. campari e lo Stile” , HQs Campari Group, Sesto San Giovanni, 2018/2019;
materials: Nattè di seta; piquet di cotone a piccoli motivi in rilievo stampato a righe h. cm. 5; crinolina di nylon; gazaar di seta; canneté di seta.
remark: Il capo evoca leggerezza e movimento e il gioco delle righe su una forma circolare crea un effetto di geometrie inaspettate
llm_description: Fashion illustration of a black top with wide sleeves and a red and white striped skirt.

source_path: ferre-rag-model/Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1988 SS/Womenswear/Fashion show drawings/34411.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (scritta oro)
acquisition: Spazio FGF c/o Open Care - Milano
description: Abito al ginocchio con scollo all’americana che avvolge il collo con accenni di revers e che lascia scoperta la schiena. Nella parte anteriore l’abito presenta un accenno di baschina, con effetto marsina, staccata rispetto all’abito e sostenuta dalla crinolina di nylon, che prende avvio dalla parte posteriore dei fianchi. Chiusura posteriore con zip e bottoni automatici ricoperti.
materials: Pizzo macramé di cotone; crinolina di nylon; chiffon di seta.
remark: In un voluto gioco di contrasti si sommano la contemporaneità della silhouette con ispirazioni vagamente maschili, il rigore del nero assoluto, la preziosità del pizzo macramé e gli effetti trompe-l’oeil.
working_process: Applicazioni all over di fiori ricamati
llm_description: Sketch of a knee-length dress with a halter neckline and a basque waist, featuring floral embroidery and a trompe l'oeil effect.

source_path: ferre-rag-model/Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1988 SS/Womenswear/Fashion show drawings/33552.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré Couture (scritta oro)
description: Abito formato da due parti tra loro sovrapposte. La prima, interna e a bustier, è di linea fasciante e ha una lunghezza sopra il ginocchio. La seconda, esterna e costruita a ruota, è lunga alla caviglia e montata in sbieco. Grazie alle impunture, il tessuto tartan è assemblato a un leggero strato di ovattina, così da sostenere il volume e la corposità della ruota. E’ presente un sotto-abito in crinolina in cui sono accostati ra loro diversi segmenti circolari che seguono l’andamento del capo. Nella parte inferiore, è posizionata un’alta balza mossa da profonde pieghe, che enfatizza ulteriormente il volume.
exhibitions: “Tartan”, FIT, New York, 1988
materials: Taffetà di seta tinto in filo a motivo tartan color rosso, giallo, verde e bluette; piquet di cotone bianco; crinolina di nylon nero; piquet di cotone bianco
remark: L’anima di questo abito è più che mai sfaccettata, singolare, curiosa. In esso si sommano i colori vivissimi della Martinica, accostati però tra loro in una delle fantasie più tradizionali e antiche dell’Europa, il tartan. Non solo: se il colore pieno esprime il piacere estremo per la fantasia, la gioia, lo slancio, la precisione della struttura porta in sé tutta l’importanza della geometria, dell’architettura, della perizia sartoriale.
working_process: Impunture parallele a 1 cm
llm_description: Fashion illustration of a dress with a tartan pattern and a black element.

Grazie alle impunture, il tessuto tartan è assemblato a un leggero strato di ovattina, così da sostenere il volume e la corposità della ruota. E’ presente un sotto-abito in crinolina in cui sono accostati ra loro diversi segmenti circolari che seguono l’andamento del capo. Nella parte inferiore, è posizionata un’alta balza mossa da profonde pieghe, che enfatizza ulteriormente il volume.
exhibitions: “Tartan”, FIT, New York, 1988
materials: Taffetà di seta tinto in filo a motivo tartan color rosso, giallo, verde e bluette; piquet di cotone bianco; crinolina di nylon nero; piquet di cotone bianco
remark: L’anima di questo abito è più che mai sfaccettata, singolare, curiosa. In esso si sommano i colori vivissimi della Martinica, accostati però tra loro in una delle fantasie più tradizionali e antiche dell’Europa, il tartan. Non solo: se il colore pieno esprime il piacere estremo per la fantasia, la gioia, lo slancio, la precisione della struttura porta in sé tutta l’importanza della geometria, dell’architettura, della perizia sartoriale.
working_process: Impunture parallele a 1 cm
llm_description: Strapless dress with a white bodice and a tartan-patterned overskirt, featuring a black underskirt with white flounces; black bow at the waist.

source_path: ferre-rag-model/Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1988 SS/Womenswear/Fashion show photos/47619.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (scritta oro)
description: The dress has two parts, an inner one and an outer one. The inner part is a bustier-dress mid thigh in length made from eight cuts of cloth. The four-whalebone back zip bustier features horizontal rows of topstitching 1 cm apart. The outside of the dress, which is sewn onto the upper part of the bustier, consists of four empty circles all in different sizes. Two circles originate halfway in back, move toward and then rotate at the front before returning to the starting point. Meanwhile, a medium size circle starts from the mid front and performs the same type of rotation in the opposite direction. Albeit sewn on simply to the bustier, too, the largest circle is free to drape, so forming voluminous spirals at various heights.
exhibitions: “Gianfranco Ferré Designs”, Phoenix Art Museum, Phoenix, 2015
materials: Silk gazaar: nylon crinoline; nylon tulle
present_location: FGF space c/o Open Care - Milan
remark: With this creation Gianfranco Ferré attained a unique sense of levity, as if a warm breeze were blowing and from time to time a swirl of air overcame the force of gravity. Thus the dress shifts gently forward, in a plastic movement guaranteed by the delicacy of the gazar fabric.
working_process: Parallel stitching cm 1
llm_description: Strapless white dress with a mid-thigh length inner bustier and an outer layer of four circular, draped panels creating a voluminous, spiraling effect; accessorized with black gloves and a headpiece.

llm_description: Strapless white dress with a mid-thigh length inner bustier and an outer layer of four circular, draped panels creating a voluminous, spiraling effect; accessorized with black gloves and a headpiece.

source_path: ferre-rag-model/Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1988 SS/Womenswear/Fashion show photos/55115.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (scritta oro)
description: The dress has two parts, an inner one and an outer one. The inner part is a bustier-dress mid thigh in length made from eight cuts of cloth. The four-whalebone back zip bustier features horizontal rows of topstitching 1 cm apart. The outside of the dress, which is sewn onto the upper part of the bustier, consists of four empty circles all in different sizes. Two circles originate halfway in back, move toward and then rotate at the front before returning to the starting point. Meanwhile, a medium size circle starts from the mid front and performs the same type of rotation in the opposite direction. Albeit sewn on simply to the bustier, too, the largest circle is free to drape, so forming voluminous spirals at various heights.
exhibitions: “Gianfranco Ferré Designs”, Phoenix Art Museum, Phoenix, 2015
materials: Silk gazaar: nylon crinoline; nylon tulle
present_location: FGF space c/o Open Care - Milan
remark: With this creation Gianfranco Ferré attained a unique sense of levity, as if a warm breeze were blowing and from time to time a swirl of air overcame the force of gravity. Thus the dress shifts gently forward, in a plastic movement guaranteed by the delicacy of the gazar fabric.

source_path: ferre-rag-model/Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1989 SS/Womenswear/Fashion show drawings/35363.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
description: Jacket spencer style with one-button single breasting. Extrawide lapels, long slender sleeves with overlay slit cuff, padded bombé shoulders. Skirt with high waist. Straight cut, knee length. T-shirt with low-cut scoop neckline. Short-sleeve slimfit style. Flowers in soft ivory and straw yellow shades of silk organza, silk organza, lace and raffia.
materials: Straw color tussah silk with partial tone-on-tone raffia embroidery (Forster Willi, Switzerland; art. 99584, col. MM10), nude color silk crêpe de chine (Taroni, Como; art. 16849, art. 3049/bis), floral design ivory silk & gold lurex lace (Hurel, France; art. 78779/2, var. ORPAL)
remark: This outfit has all the elegance and appeal of a 1980s women’s power suit. The short spencer with wide lapels, the strong (albeit not boxy) shoulders, the slimfit T-shirt and knee-skimming skirt capture the concept to perfection. By contrast, what creates the amazing sense of magic and uniqueness is the material mix – a sum total of lusciously light silk, lace & tone-on- tone embroidery – with the quietly sunny monochrome straw hue heightening the impact in the finest of ways. The oneiric quality of the look culminates in the stunning floral ornamention at front waist. In the final analysis, this latter element becomes the true focal point of the ensemble, in stark opposition to the overall classy simplicity of shape.
llm_description: Sketch of a jacket and skirt outfit with floral embellishments at the waist.

source_path: ferre-rag-model/Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1989 SS/Womenswear/Fashion show drawings/49147.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
description: Tunic A-line style. Scoop neckline, 7/8 length. Construction featuring the hand application of lace, three layers of (different) fabric sewn together with 1 cm parallel topstitching. Skirt with straight line, no waistband. Same type of construction as for tunic. Hat in natural straw. Large size, dome style. Hand sewn. Flowers in silk organza.
materials: Silk lace in lovely bois de rose hue, taffeta with sweet bouquet design, garland & flower decors (Ottolenghi, Milano), bois de rose silk tulle (Zecchini, Milano), black nylon tulle
remark: While lines are essentially simple – as in gently flaring tunic and straight knee-skimming skirt – this outfit succeeds in capturing all the aristo allure of once-upon-a-time lingerie, elegantly sensual, exquisitely seductive though not openly sexy. What works the special magic is the delicate bois de rose hue. Yet most of all the trick is in the airy lightness of the fundamental material, the tulle that is taken to the next level on the fascination front thanks to the gorgeous lace appliquéwork, the fine horizontal topstitching, the garland & flower ornamentation, the sublime layering with taffeta.
working_process: Lace in appliqué form; parallel topstitching with 1 cm spacing
llm_description: Sketch of a tunic and skirt outfit with a scoop neckline and 7/8 length sleeves, paired with a dome-style hat.

source_path: ferre-rag-model/Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1989 SS/Womenswear/Fashion show drawings/16563.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
description: Duster in ultra soft and fluid A line, ankle length. Strong shoulders, magnificent puff sleeves with silk organza layer in matching color. Pants in coral cady. Extrawide leg bottom, deep unpressed pleat. Semi-hidden pockets. Belt with fabric covering, rectangular gold chrome buckle. Top brassière style in coral cady with adhesive element. Hat in natural straw, color black. Large size, dome style. Hand sewn.
materials: Coral color silk cady (Taroni, Como; art. 16691, col. 4002), silk organza (Taroni, Como)
present_location: FGF space c/o Open Care - Milan
remark: Despite two characteristics contradicting the idea – namely, the lack of a hood and the presence of lavish gigot sleeves – this cloak-duster is ideally a revisitation of the Maghrebian burnus (or alsham). The designer’s intent is definitely there in the luscious shoulder-to-floor draping and in the rich flaring toward the bottom. The trousers capture the same sense of soft fluidity reminiscent of Arabian garb. The chromatic uniformity of the ensemble’s two key pieces enhances and exalts the stylistic coherence, the underlying inspiration too.
working_process: Openwork (ajour embroidery) edging
llm_description: Sketch of a duster and pants outfit featuring a wide-brimmed hat, with the duster rendered in red and the hat in black.

source_path: ferre-rag-model/Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1989 SS/Womenswear/Fashion show drawings/35372.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
description: Jacket in 7/8 length with straight line, round neck. Rich puff sleeves with silk organza layer on the inside. Skirt in silk cady. Straight line with narrow waistband. Floor length, back slit. Top debardeur style in silk cady. Blousy effect, hip-hugging basque. Belt in Vienna straw, with matching rectangular leather buckle.
materials: Silk marocain with panel type print (Ratti, Como; art. 0457, des. F69413, var. 41), black silk cady (Taroni, Como; art. 16691)
remark: While stopping well above the knee, this jacket captures all the flair of the magnificent mid 19th century evening cloaks (1830s to ‘50s) typically complementing full-length body-skimming fourreaux. What both the original versions and Ferré’s revisitation have in common are two primary features: firstly, the gorgeous fabric (here a fabulous silk marocain) that gives structure and emphasis to a design basically simple of construction; and secondly, the lavish beauty of the soft puff sleeves. Equally important in creating the sense of richness is the intentional decorative complexity and utter sophistication of the print motif, namely Vienna-strawlike rosettes making a stunning impression on a black background and then intensifying of intricacy on the sleeves (in a twofold manner: plain tone for one part, paisley pattern for the other).
llm_description: Sketch of a jacket and skirt outfit featuring a 7/8 length jacket with puff sleeves and a floor-length skirt with a back slit.

What both the original versions and Ferré’s revisitation have in common are two primary features: firstly, the gorgeous fabric (here a fabulous silk marocain) that gives structure and emphasis to a design basically simple of construction; and secondly, the lavish beauty of the soft puff sleeves. Equally important in creating the sense of richness is the intentional decorative complexity and utter sophistication of the print motif, namely Vienna-strawlike rosettes making a stunning impression on a black background and then intensifying of intricacy on the sleeves (in a twofold manner: plain tone for one part, paisley pattern for the other).
llm_description: A black and gold printed jacket with red trim and wide sleeves.

source_path: ferre-rag-model/Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1989 SS/Womenswear/Fashion show drawings/16566.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
description: Evening dress in four pieces: bodice, skirt, petticoat, sash. Bodice in black silk crêpe. Slim fit with narrow straps, back zip/clasp closure. Skirt in black tulle with black/red/pink/yellow/sky blue/green bias-cut silk gazar flounces. Clasp/snap fastening at front waist, opening onto an underskirt. Petticoat in print silk crêpe (same colors as skirt). Slim line, knee length, small back slit, side zip/button closure. Sash in matching print silk organza. To knot at waist. Black crepe lining for bodice. Stiff nylon understructure & black satin lining for skirt. Black crêpe lining for petticoat.
exhibitions: “Altre Emozioni”, Firenze, 2000; “Moda tra analogie e dissonanze”, Firenze, 2010
materials: Silk crepe (Taroni, Como; art. 16691, col. 120), silk gazar (Clerici Tessuto, Como; art. 1234, col. 398, 470, 205, 298, 198, 122, black), print silk crepe (Lucchini, Milano; art. 6621-67, col. 81), print silk organza (Clerici Tessuto, Como; art. 1234, col.9 & Lucchini, Milano; art. 6621, col.74
remark: What sets this ensemble a class apart is the vibrant Mediterranean allure that comes through in the skirt’s strong chromatic impact and graceful ease. Contrasting nicely with the simplicity and severity of the plain black top are the bright colors of the bayadère stripe-type flounces along the base of the long full black tulle outer skirt. The same upbeat palette appears in print form on the short slim crepe underskirt. To complete the sensation of exuberant vitality there’s a sash knotted at the waist. Ever an emblem of fetching Mediterranean femininity, the sash heightens the sinuous grace of swaying hips. Donation to the Pitti Palace Costume Gallery in Florence (2000).
bibliography: “Gianfranco Ferré. I quaderni di Pitti 01”, Giunti Editore, Firenze, 2000
llm_description: Fashion illustration of a four-piece evening outfit, featuring a black top and a skirt with multicolored horizontal stripes.

source_path: ferre-rag-model/Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1989 SS/Womenswear/Fashion show drawings/35377.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
description: Long jacket, round neck, with a slim line; kissed closure up to the waist; elbow-length sleeves. Embroidery with horizontal baiadera stripes. The embroidery motif is composed as follows (from top to bottom). At the top of the shoulder, a black band composed of: 2 rows of jais, 4 rows of beads, 1 row alternating 1 canottiglia and 3 beads, 1 row alternating 3 larger black beads and one gold bead of the same size. This is followed by 1 row of gold beads and 1 row of gold jais. The green section continues with 1 row of jais and 1 row of canottiglie. Another gold section follows: 1 row of canottiglie, 1 row of jais alternating gold/ivory, 1 row of canottiglie. Then, a red section descends from the shoulders to the chest, composed as follows: 1 row of canottiglie, 1 row of beads arranged to form what appears to be a tube with vertical stripes, 1 row of jais of large dimensions, 1 row of large smooth round beads and one of large irregular beads, 1 more row of beads arranged in a tube, 3 rows of irregular beads occasionally interspersed with black beads, 1 row of tube beads alternating 2 rows of red and 2 rows of black, 1 row composed of jais with pendulums resting on paillettes of ivory color, 1 row of beads alternating 1 red and 3 black, 2 rows of red beads, 1 row of large jais. Subsequently, 1 gold section composed of 1 row of wooden beads and 3 rows of decreasing-sized gold beads. 1 green section composed of 1 row of beads, of two different sizes alternating light green and dark green, and 1 row of transparent colored green glass beads. This is followed by a pink section: 1 row of canottiglie, 1 row of colored glass beads, 1 row of canottiglie. This is followed by another green section: 1 row of beads with pendulums resting on paillettes of ivory color, 1 row of square jais, 1 row of round beads. A row of wooden beads closes the chest area. Then, a red section follows: 1 row of round beads, 1 row of square jais, 1 row of beads arranged to form what appears to be a tube with vertical stripes, 1 row of horizontal canottiglie, 1 row of beads surrounded by visible metallic support. This is followed by a green section: 1 row of horizontal canottiglie, 1 row of vertical canottiglie from which beads hang resting on paillettes of ivory color, 1 row of horizontal canottiglie, 1 row of transparent colored glass beads, 1 row of vertical canottiglie. A repeat row of wooden beads marks the beginning of the waist. 1 row of black beads and 1 row of smaller black beads. 1 pink section composed of: 1 row of horizontal canottiglie, 1 row of jais shaped like a drop, 1 row of transparent glass beads. 3 successive rows of gold beads leading to 1 row of horizontal rectangular red canottiglie. Next, a weave of wooden beads from which green-colored beads hang, starting a new dark green section: jais, 2 rows of beads, 1 row of pearls alternating 2 dark greens and 1 black, 1 row alternating green, pink, and red paillettes. The waist is then marked by a row alternating 3 wooden beads in coral color and 1 green bead. This is followed by a large green section: 1 row of vertical canottiglie, 1 row of horizontal canottiglie from which beads hang resting on paillettes of ivory color, 1 additional row of densely packed vertical canottiglie, 1 row of transparent colored beads. This is followed by a gold section: 1 row of horizontal canottiglie, 1 with a crossed pattern between silver jais, gold beads, and wood. This is followed by a large black section composed of: 1 row of canottiglie, 1 band with a triangular pattern created by gold beads bordered by red beads from which 3 movable red canottiglie hang, 7 rows of black beads, 1 row of horizontal black canottiglie, 3 rows of black beads, 1 with a crossed pattern between purple jais, black beads, and wood, 6 rows of black beads, 1 row of horizontal canottiglie. Next, 4 rows of wooden beads, 2 of green beads, and again 3 of wooden beads. The jacket continues towards the end with a red section: 1 row of square jais, 1 row of pearls, 1 row of beads alternating red and black with pearls that hang and rest on ivory colored sequins 1 row of beads arranged to form a tube
exhibitions: “Gianfranco Ferré. Moda, un racconto nei disegni”, Centro Culturale Santa Maria della Pietà, Cremona, 2017
materials: Silk organza (Clerici, Como, art 1234 col. 1); transparent colored glass beads; sequins; jet beads; canottiglie; wooden and mother-of-pearl beads; all in the following colors: coral, red, purple, ivory, gold, peacock green, pink, silver, and black
present_location: Spazio FGF c/o Open Care - Milano
remark: The richness and uniqueness of the ensemble reside entirely in the decoration of the jacket, simple in its construction but incredibly rich in material choices and above all decorations. The baiadera stripes that animate it not only juxtapose colors in stark contrast but also alternate different decorative techniques - from hand embroidery to loom-executed embroidery overlaid on the print - giving not only a sense of opulence to the garment but also of almost three-dimensional material density. Similar, although more measured and confined to the border, is the rich decorative identity of the scarf-shawl, which, wrapped around the neck, seems to cascade carelessly down the back. The severity of the black trousers, also wide and flowing, which once again seems to pay tribute to Middle Eastern tradition, recalibrates the whole ensemble.
working_process: Allover hand loom embroidery
llm_description: Fashion illustration of a jacket with horizontal stripes and heavy embellishments, paired with black pants.

A repeat row of wooden beads marks the beginning of the waist. 1 row of black beads and 1 row of smaller black beads. 1 pink section composed of: 1 row of horizontal canottiglie, 1 row of jais shaped like a drop, 1 row of transparent glass beads. 3 successive rows of gold beads leading to 1 row of horizontal rectangular red canottiglie. Next, a weave of wooden beads from which green-colored beads hang, starting a new dark green section: jais, 2 rows of beads, 1 row of pearls alternating 2 dark greens and 1 black, 1 row alternating green, pink, and red paillettes. The waist is then marked by a row alternating 3 wooden beads in coral color and 1 green bead. This is followed by a large green section: 1 row of vertical canottiglie, 1 row of horizontal canottiglie from which beads hang resting on paillettes of ivory color, 1 additional row of densely packed vertical canottiglie, 1 row of transparent colored beads. This is followed by a gold section: 1 row of horizontal canottiglie, 1 with a crossed pattern between silver jais, gold beads, and wood. This is followed by a large black section composed of: 1 row of canottiglie, 1 band with a triangular pattern created by gold beads bordered by red beads from which 3 movable red canottiglie hang, 7 rows of black beads, 1 row of horizontal black canottiglie, 3 rows of black beads, 1 with a crossed pattern between purple jais, black beads, and wood, 6 rows of black beads, 1 row of horizontal canottiglie. Next, 4 rows of wooden beads, 2 of green beads, and again 3 of wooden beads. The jacket continues towards the end with a red section: 1 row of square jais, 1 row of pearls, 1 row of beads alternating red and black with pearls that hang and rest on ivory colored sequins 1 row of beads arranged to form a tube
exhibitions: “Gianfranco Ferré. Moda, un racconto nei disegni”, Centro Culturale Santa Maria della Pietà, Cremona, 2017
materials: Silk organza (Clerici, Como, art 1234 col. 1); transparent colored glass beads; sequins; jet beads; canottiglie; wooden and mother-of-pearl beads; all in the following colors: coral, red, purple, ivory, gold, peacock green, pink, silver, and black
present_location: Spazio FGF c/o Open Care - Milano
remark: The richness and uniqueness of the ensemble reside entirely in the decoration of the jacket, simple in its construction but incredibly rich in material choices and above all decorations. The baiadera stripes that animate it not only juxtapose colors in stark contrast but also alternate different decorative techniques - from hand embroidery to loom-executed embroidery overlaid on the print - giving not only a sense of opulence to the garment but also of almost three-dimensional material density. Similar, although more measured and confined to the border, is the rich decorative identity of the scarf-shawl, which, wrapped around the neck, seems to cascade carelessly down the back. The severity of the black trousers, also wide and flowing, which once again seems to pay tribute to Middle Eastern tradition, recalibrates the whole ensemble.
working_process: Allover hand loom embroidery
llm_description: A model wears a horizontally striped jacket heavily embellished with beads and a black shawl, paired with black pants.

source_path: ferre-rag-model/Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1989 SS/Womenswear/Fashion show drawings/16567.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (scritta oro)
object: Abito da sera NON PRESENTE IN ARCHIVIO/Donazione galleria Costume Firenze
description: Gonna in pizzo color crema doppiato in tulle e in organza di seta in tinta che avvolge la vita come una cintura; chiusura posteriore con automatici. Sul fianco sinistro sono inseriti due pannelli in pizzo color crema doppiato in tulle, sovrapposti e di dimensioni diverse, con orlo festonato, ripresi, a formare doppio piegone con doppia balza di tulle plissettato alla cintura e organza di seta color rosa e color carne. Sul fianco sinistro, applicazione di rose e ramoscelli in organza e rafia naturale, color grigio perla e rosa cipria. Fodera in organza di seta a righe rosa e color carne. Body in tulle elasticizzato color carne, ricamato con perline in varie tonalità di rosa e argento, foderato in crepe di seta in tinta. Chiusura posteriore con automatici.
exhibitions: “Moda tra analogie e dissonanze”, Firenze, 2010
materials: Organza di seta (Clerici Tessuto,Como; art. 1540/203); tulle di seta (Zecchini, Milano).
remark: La composta asimmetria dei volumi, la cui ricchezza è intenzionalmente tutta spostata lateralmente, il contrasto tra lunghezze, più che misurate nella gonna in sé e decisamente importanti nei pannelli ad inserto, diversi per dimensioni e “raccordati” alla gonna stessa da un “bouquet” floreale multimaterico, segnano l’identità fortemente architettonica, solo in apparenza “scomposta”, della costruzione del capo, senza penalizzarne la lievità, la sensazione forte di movimento che lo percorre anche grazie al gioco di plissettature a vista, né la morbida romantica anima cromatica, solare e rosata al contempo. Riflette questa aerea complessità dell’insieme il body: ridotto ai minimi termini per costruzione, è in verità assolutamente prezioso nella sua definizione decorativa. In questo completo, come in tutta la collezione, il leit motiv è costituito dall’accostamento singolare ed innovativo tra le tipologie più femminili ed aeree della seta e delle materie sostanzialmente estranee agli orizzonti dell’abbigliamento di lusso, in primis paglia di Vienna e rafia, che tuttavia riescono a creare impressioni sfarzo ed opulenza autentici. Secondo capo. il primo usato in sfilata è alla Galleria del Costume di Palazzo Pitti a Firenze ( donazione 2000)
llm_description: Fashion illustration of a dress with a fitted bodice and a draped skirt with a long panel.

source_path: ferre-rag-model/Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1989 SS/Womenswear/Technical sheets/50881.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (scritta oro)
object: Abito da sera NON PRESENTE IN ARCHIVIO/Donazione galleria Costume Firenze
description: Gonna in pizzo color crema doppiato in tulle e in organza di seta in tinta che avvolge la vita come una cintura; chiusura posteriore con automatici. Sul fianco sinistro sono inseriti due pannelli in pizzo color crema doppiato in tulle, sovrapposti e di dimensioni diverse, con orlo festonato, ripresi, a formare doppio piegone con doppia balza di tulle plissettato alla cintura e organza di seta color rosa e color carne. Sul fianco sinistro, applicazione di rose e ramoscelli in organza e rafia naturale, color grigio perla e rosa cipria. Fodera in organza di seta a righe rosa e color carne. Body in tulle elasticizzato color carne, ricamato con perline in varie tonalità di rosa e argento, foderato in crepe di seta in tinta. Chiusura posteriore con automatici.
exhibitions: “Moda tra analogie e dissonanze”, Firenze, 2010
materials: Organza di seta (Clerici Tessuto,Como; art. 1540/203); tulle di seta (Zecchini, Milano).
remark: La composta asimmetria dei volumi, la cui ricchezza è intenzionalmente tutta spostata lateralmente, il contrasto tra lunghezze, più che misurate nella gonna in sé e decisamente importanti nei pannelli ad inserto, diversi per dimensioni e “raccordati” alla gonna stessa da un “bouquet” floreale multimaterico, segnano l’identità fortemente architettonica, solo in apparenza “scomposta”, della costruzione del capo, senza penalizzarne la lievità, la sensazione forte di movimento che lo percorre anche grazie al gioco di plissettature a vista, né la morbida romantica anima cromatica, solare e rosata al contempo. Riflette questa aerea complessità dell’insieme il body: ridotto ai minimi termini per costruzione, è in verità assolutamente prezioso nella sua definizione decorativa. In questo completo, come in tutta la collezione, il leit motiv è costituito dall’accostamento singolare ed innovativo tra le tipologie più femminili ed aeree della seta e delle materie sostanzialmente estranee agli orizzonti dell’abbigliamento di lusso, in primis paglia di Vienna e rafia, che tuttavia riescono a creare impressioni sfarzo ed opulenza autentici. Secondo capo. il primo usato in sfilata è alla Galleria del Costume di Palazzo Pitti a Firenze ( donazione 2000)
llm_description: Technical drawing and fabric swatches for a dress with a beaded bustier and a lace skirt with a draped panel and floral embellishments.

source_path: ferre-rag-model/Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1989 SS/Womenswear/Fashion show drawings/49164.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré Couture (scritta oro)
description: Abito a bustier di linea dritta lungo alla caviglia con taglio sotto il seno, realizzato in cady di seta. avorio. I due pannelli da cui è formato si uniscono sul davanti e sul dietro. La cucitura posteriore si apre a formare uno spacco a partire dall’incavo del ginocchio a terra e nella parte superiore è inserita la chiusura-lampo. L’abito è foderato in crêpe de chine avorio. Cabane di linea svasata con maniche a kimono, lungo al secondo fianco. L’ampiezza delle maniche è raccolta al fondo formando pieghe trattenute da un polsino con chiusura ad asola e bottone ricoperto. Il capo presenta in ogni sua parte un complesso ricamo in fili dorati e viscosa a volute barocche intrecciate tra loro, arricchite da motivi floreali. L’orlo irregolare a smerli segue l’andamento delle volute. La chiusura frontale è assicurata da un bottone automatico ricoperto.
exhibitions: Gianfranco Ferré e Maria Luigia: Inattese Assonanze”, Palazzo del Governatore, Parma, 2016; “Gianfranco Ferré. Moda, un racconto nei disegni”, Centro Culturale Santa Maria della Pietà, Cremona, 2017;
materials: Rete diagonale in lurex oro; tulle di nylon avorio; filo di lurex spiralato oro; filo di viscosa; paillettes argento; jais oro e legno; georgette di seta avorio; cady di seta avorio; crêpe de chine avorio
remark: Alla semplicità dell’abito si contrappone lo sfarzo di impronta barocca del caban, con le volute che richiamano i legni incurvati e gli intrecci in stile “Thonet”. L’enfasi è accentuata dalla preziosità delle applicazioni in jais e dai ricami, che contribuiscono a dare un’impressione di femminità sontuosa.
working_process: Sovrapposizione di tessuti (tulle, nylon e lurex) uniti tra loro da ricamo a mano con impunture parallele in filo lurex spiralato; ricamo a mano a volute e fiori con effetto rilievo
llm_description: Sketch of a caban featuring baroque volutes and floral motifs, with gold and brown shading.

source_path: ferre-rag-model/Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1989 SS/Womenswear/Fashion show drawings/16557.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
description: Two-piece evening dress in sandy beige silk taffeta. Slim whalebone bodice with wide band along strapless neckline all about embroidery and other lavish ornamentation (gold sequins, gold metallic mesh, gold beads, organza flowers, raffia spikelets). Back zip closure. Long straight skirt with back slit. Bustier-belt in tulle, raffia and gold metallic mesh with rich embroidery & beading (fine gold silk cord, gold and wood beads). Wide swingy basque, spiral edging. Back snap closure. Shawl accessory.
materials: Silk taffeta (Taroni, Como; art. 16840, col. 94), organza flowers/twigs and raffia spikelets (“Moda e Guarnizioni” art. 9808 - 126 col. gold)
remark: An intentional trompe l’oeil effect defines the heart & soul of this evening dress. The bodice is actually a wide bustier-belt mimicking an exquisite piece of sculpture or a supremely feminine type of armor with beautifully swirling basque. This fabulous creation – complete with amazing floral decoration – complements an airy light and absolutely fluid silk taffeta skirt. This is the second prototype. The original runway one is on display at the Pitti Palace Costume Gallery in Florence (donation in 2000).
llm_description: Fashion illustration of a strapless dress with a fitted bodice and long skirt, featuring embellishments at the neckline and a flowing silhouette.

source_path: ferre-rag-model/Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1989 SS/Womenswear/Fashion show drawings/16556.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
description: Dress in layers of silk georgette. Empire style, long trumpet skirt. Torchon crossover band at bustline setting in motion deep unpressed pleat all the way down to asymmetric hem: ankle skimming in front, with small train in back. Bolero with elaborate gold thread openwork/spiral embroidery in front. Perfectly symmetric lapels. Semi sheer mid back thanks to layers of tulle & mesh topstitched together with a delicate gold thread. At back neck and base of bolero two pairs of ringlets. Small silk flowers on shoulders, short sleeves and part of front.
exhibitions: “Ferré. Abito come accento che esce dal corpo”, area Bernardelli, Mantova, 2015; “Gianfranco Ferré. Moda, un racconto nei disegni”, Centro Culturale Santa Maria della Pietà, Cremona, 2017;
materials: Black silk georgette (Clerici, Como; art. 1031, col. 9), beige silk organza (Clerici, Como), raffia, gold lurex diagonal mesh, stiff white nylon tulle, gold lurex spiral thread, iridescent beige silk taffeta
present_location: FGF space c/o Open Care - Milan
remark: An exquisitely simple black evening dress with asymmetric flaring toward bottom finds an element of distinction in the torchon effect that gives definition to an empire line. By contrast, the sumptuous little jacket bordering on a bolero sparks images of a precious English Tudor or Spanish jerkin. It’s all about the spirals reminiscent of the famous Thonet Style (wood chairs) that become all the more luxurious thanks to the chunky flower decors, inserts & ringlets in a variety of materials. Nicely offsetting this quasi-baroque impact is the transparency in back.
working_process: Hand application of silk flowers; spiral thread embroidery/topstitching (by hand) on tulle, nylon and lurex, so joining the three fabrics in layers; hand embroidery in raffia, complete with relief effect; hand embroidery (in openwork pattern) of gold lurex spiral thread.
llm_description: Fashion illustration of a black evening dress with a trumpet skirt and an asymmetric hem, paired with a gold bolero.

source_path: ferre-rag-model/Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1989 SS/Womenswear/Fashion show photos – Milan Castello Sforzesco/58475.jpg
asset_type: Fashion show photos – Milan Castello Sforzesco
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
description: Dress in layers of silk georgette. Empire style, long trumpet skirt. Torchon crossover band at bustline setting in motion deep unpressed pleat all the way down to asymmetric hem: ankle skimming in front, with small train in back. Bolero with elaborate gold thread openwork/spiral embroidery in front. Perfectly symmetric lapels. Semi sheer mid back thanks to layers of tulle & mesh topstitched together with a delicate gold thread. At back neck and base of bolero two pairs of ringlets. Small silk flowers on shoulders, short sleeves and part of front.
exhibitions: “Ferré. Abito come accento che esce dal corpo”, area Bernardelli, Mantova, 2015; “Gianfranco Ferré. Moda, un racconto nei disegni”, Centro Culturale Santa Maria della Pietà, Cremona, 2017;
materials: Black silk georgette (Clerici, Como; art. 1031, col. 9), beige silk organza (Clerici, Como), raffia, gold lurex diagonal mesh, stiff white nylon tulle, gold lurex spiral thread, iridescent beige silk taffeta
present_location: FGF space c/o Open Care - Milan
remark: An exquisitely simple black evening dress with asymmetric flaring toward bottom finds an element of distinction in the torchon effect that gives definition to an empire line. By contrast, the sumptuous little jacket bordering on a bolero sparks images of a precious English Tudor or Spanish jerkin. It’s all about the spirals reminiscent of the famous Thonet Style (wood chairs) that become all the more luxurious thanks to the chunky flower decors, inserts & ringlets in a variety of materials. Nicely offsetting this quasi-baroque impact is the transparency in back.
working_process: Hand application of silk flowers; spiral thread embroidery/topstitching (by hand) on tulle, nylon and lurex, so joining the three fabrics in layers; hand embroidery in raffia, complete with relief effect; hand embroidery (in openwork pattern) of gold lurex spiral thread.
llm_description: A black evening dress with a trumpet skirt, paired with a gold bolero featuring floral embellishments, and matching gloves.

source_path: ferre-rag-model/Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1989 SS/Womenswear/Fashion show drawings/33547.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
description: Evening ensemble in two pieces: Short bustier style dress with slim line, above-the-knee length. Big flowers in lacquered silk all over the bodice. Floor-length skirt, ¾ circular style, tied onto the dress with a topstitched silk ribbon. Base part in three layers of tulle topstitched together, then covered lavishly with mesh & point d’esprit veil flounces. Silk organza lining.
materials: Wool/silk (Taroni, Como; art. 17055), point d’ésprit veil (Solstiss, Paris; art. 225026), mesh (Rodolph Simon, Paris; art. 53229), horsehair ribbon, nylon tulle, silk organza (Clerici Tessuto, Como)
present_location: open care
remark: The actual dress, for the most part a short simple sheath, comes gloriously to life thanks to the big floral decors on the bodice. They create the impression of a magical garden as rich and luxurious as the long flowing detachable skirt open in front. What makes the latter so sumptuous is the array of layers & flounces that spark images of 19th century petticoats. Meanwhile, although usually the utmost expression of severity elegance, here black accentuates the sense of opulence. Also by capturing glints of light.
working_process: Application of handmade flowers in lacquered silk and organza; 1 cm parallel toptitching on tulle and veil, so joining the materials in layers; point d’esprit veil and mesh ruffles in horizontal parallel flounce form
llm_description: Sketch of a two-piece evening dress featuring a short bustier-style dress with floral embellishments on the bodice and a floor-length, circular skirt.

present_location: open care
remark: The actual dress, for the most part a short simple sheath, comes gloriously to life thanks to the big floral decors on the bodice. They create the impression of a magical garden as rich and luxurious as the long flowing detachable skirt open in front. What makes the latter so sumptuous is the array of layers & flounces that spark images of 19th century petticoats. Meanwhile, although usually the utmost expression of severity elegance, here black accentuates the sense of opulence. Also by capturing glints of light.
working_process: Application of handmade flowers in lacquered silk and organza; 1 cm parallel toptitching on tulle and veil, so joining the materials in layers; point d’esprit veil and mesh ruffles in horizontal parallel flounce form
llm_description: Material swatches showing black point d’ésprit veil and mesh.

source_path: ferre-rag-model/Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1989 SS/Womenswear/Fashion show drawings/16568.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
description: Evening dress in three pieces: Bodice in black silk gros grain. Construction in seven small panels diminishing in size from center outward. Whalebone at seams. Topstitched black faille waistband. Back zip closure. Flounce skirt open in front. In ecru color tulle. Doubling in ivory lace with floral embroidery. Faux-raffia nylon ruffles. Ecru silk organza lining. Underskirt in ecru silk crepe with doubling in lace (same as skirt). Knee length with semi scallop-edge hem. Side zip closure. Organza rose decor.
exhibitions: “Altre emozioni”, Palazzo Pitti, Firenze, 2000; “Moda tra analogie e dissonanze”, Firenze, 2010
materials: Silk gros grain (Redaelli, Milano; art. Super Ivonne), lace (Ricamificio Zibetti, Gallarate; art. 1156), nylon (“Moda e Guarnizioni” - art. 53), silk organza (Taroni, Como), silk crepe
remark: Despite the simple, straightforward image of this contemporary low-cut top, the construction process is definitely complex. Whatever, all the visual, material & decorative opulence is in the bottom part of the dress, both in the short slim (albeit ultra precious) underskirt and in the sumptuous flounce skirt so wonderfully reminiscent of 19th century petticoats. Complete with rich embroidery & ruffles, the primary skirt finds a further note of novelty in the reinvention of nylon mesh in the form of woven Vienna straw. Donation to the Pitti Palace Costume Gallery in Florence (2000)
bibliography: “Gianfranco Ferré. I quaderni di Pitti 01”, Giunti Editore, Firenze, 2000
llm_description: Sketch of a three-piece evening dress featuring a bustier top and a flounce skirt, with an underskirt. The sketch highlights the construction and design details of the garment.
